test.notes: Full suite exited with only the 2 known pre-existing failures documented in AGENTS.md: aiOrchestratorService single-lane integration closing strategy and orchestratorService unified-attempt laneId handling. No additional failures were observed (1119 passed, 2 failed).

lint.notes: Lint is not configured in the project manifest.

blockingIssues.description: VAL-ARCH-004 remains unmet: although `getFullMissionView` was added in the IPC layer, the renderer/store still loads mission selection data through the legacy waterfall (`missions.get`, `orchestrator.listRuns`, `orchestrator.getRunGraph`, `orchestrator.listArtifacts`, `orchestrator.listWorkerCheckpoints`) instead of using the consolidated call.

VAL-ARCH-007 remains unmet: `MissionsPage.tsx` still owns refresh/debounce timers via `window.setTimeout` and directly registers mission/orchestrator event subscriptions, so polling/event orchestration was not fully moved into the store/shared coordinator layer.

VAL-ARCH-008 remains unmet: major containers such as `MissionTabContainer`, `MissionHeader`, and `MissionsPage` subscribe to broad sets of store slices/actions, so unrelated updates can still rerender large UI sections instead of being isolated by fine-grained selectors.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to reflect the decomposed MissionsPage/MissionChatV2 architecture and document that chat message virtualization already flows through `AgentChatMessageList` while sidebar virtualization lives in `MissionSidebar`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to record agent-browser startup/interaction evidence explicitly in handoffs, and mark missing browser validation as a procedure deviation instead of allowing `followedProcedure: true` with only test/typecheck/grep verification.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The missions-store-and-decomposition review found that the UI feature handoff listed only vitest/tsc/wc/grep commands and no `npm run dev`, agent-browser usage, or interactive checks even though the current ui-worker skill says browser validation is the primary UI verification path.
